html:
Motion direction
Demo steps
⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: five identical wireframe pinhole camera models in a horizontal row
solid black dot representing the pinhole of each camera
thick red horizontal arrows pointing right, centered beneath each camera, all of equal length

interactions.label: Motion direction
interactions.teaches: Reverses the direction of the camera's lateral translation, demonstrating that the image motion in the scene will flip directions as well.

interactions.label: Show translation sequence
interactions.teaches: Toggles between showing just a single camera and the full sequence of stepped camera positions, emphasizing that the motion is continuous over time.

interactions.label: Number of frames
interactions.teaches: Adjusts how finely the translation is sampled — more frames make the motion appear smoother, illustrating the stepwise buildup of lateral shift.

demo_steps.title: Single frame: initial position
demo_steps.narration: Here's the camera at its starting location. We'll study how a lateral translation looks as the camera moves sideways — this is like panning through a scene out the window of a moving car.

demo_steps.title: Sequence of lateral translations
demo_steps.narration: Now, see the whole motion: the camera is duplicated along the rightward path, with red arrows showing the direction and equal step size between each position. This is a pure side-to-side translation with no rotation.

demo_steps.title: Reverse motion direction
demo_steps.narration: Flip the arrow to the left — now the camera moves in the opposite direction! This reversal helps make clear that the direction of motion in the image will also flip, a key property of lateral movement.

demo_steps.title: Increase frame count: finer motion
demo_steps.narration: Let’s add more steps. With seven camera positions, the translation appears more continuous. This mirrors what happens as we take video at higher frame rates — the smoothness of the perceived motion improves, but the overall path is unchanged.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Orthographic front view centered on the row of cameras, wide enough to fit all cameras and arrows even at max frame count. Slight vertical offset so arrows are easily visible below cameras.
interactionPlan.notes: Synchronize the position of all cameras and arrows on every interaction. Arrows should always initiate at each pinhole and extend horizontally in the toggled direction. Render only one camera and arrow if showAllSteps is false; duplicate and space uniformly if true. Keep all elements crisp and lines bold for visibility.

Motion direction
Demo steps

discrepancies: Shape of the camera figures and arrows are more elongated and less detailed than in the source.
The arrows are not centered between cameras as in the reference.
The camera figures appear taller in the screenshot compared to the source.
evaluation.notes: Decent interaction and control options but some deviations in proportions and element alignment.
action_items: Adjust the scale of the camera figures to match the reference.
Ensure that arrows are centered between camera figures.
Fine-tune the camera figure dimensions to reduce height and match the original proportions.

feedback.rationale: The failure mode is limited to scale issues, and the high concept accuracy and label quality imply the plan is correct but needs execution adjustments.
action_items: Adjust the scale of the camera figures to match the reference.
Ensure that arrows are centered between camera figures.
Fine-tune the camera figure dimensions to reduce height and match the original proportions.
